scopeNote: Recovery-review is clean on the current propagated stack because every linked scrutiny review artifact now points at a commit id that both resolves and is ancestor-reachable from `recovery/08-engineer-shareout`; stale pre-restack anchors were replaced with the latest reachable propagated equivalents so the restored pass state is auditable rather than a carried-forward clean summary from older ancestry.

appliedUpdates.description: Re-anchored every stale non-ancestor recovery-review artifact to a current-stack reachable commit id: O2C `140ae266b089673dbdbe724b60946827c6afffe9`, P2P `39b73910e061a42deb23ddc16779b9be726e57f9`, corrections/control `02654ad7f839da7ffc24d0fa426904001f5acbe2`, portal `db2586fc76a6b64c1e413669906e56598d15ecca`, final-hardening `d23648b84e25955284f315c31cd3abacaec6f0d9`, and the docs-only PR #102 shareout review `5e2ef405f6c9ae47c0f7f5f08bf5c8c273bb3e66`; the shared-ci baseline and truth-rails anchors were already reachable and remained unchanged. The milestone pass therefore reflects the live propagated ancestry rather than a stale clean summary from an older restack state.

Recorded the aggregate traceability verification result explicitly: all eight linked recovery-review artifacts now point at commitIds that both resolve and are branch-reachable on `recovery/08-engineer-shareout`, so the reported 8/8 pass count does not overstate replayable evidence.

rejectedObservations.observation: The scrutiny-feature-reviewer Task droid failed to launch for every recovery-review feature with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-medium`, so all feature reviews were completed manually from mission handoffs, progress logs, and commit diffs.

The P2P review surfaced a runtime-probe contract mismatch between `.factory/services.yaml` and `testing/ci/test_pr_review_ci_packet.py`, but resolving it would require a non-additive manifest change outside this validator packet and outside the required recovery-review validator surface.
